report_metadata.cancer_type: Parotid MALT Lymphoma (Extranodal Marginal Zone Lymphoma of Mucosa-Associated Lymphoid Tissue)

treatments.name: Involved-Field Radiation Therapy (24-30 Gy)

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple retrospective series with long-term follow-up; NCCN Category 2A for Stage IE MALT lymphoma; 5-year LC rate 100% in most series; standard of care for localized EMZL

survival_benefit.rationale: 5-year OS 96.6%, PFS 82.2%, local control 100% in largest series; long-term possible cure for Stage IE disease; t(11;18)+ tumors are radiation-sensitive despite antibiotic resistance

accessibility.rationale: Standard radiation therapy available at all cancer centers; well-established dose protocols (24-30 Gy in 12-15 fractions); outpatient treatment

safety_profile.rationale: Low-dose radiation (24-30 Gy) with limited field; main toxicity is xerostomia from parotid irradiation (already affected by Sjogren's); may worsen existing dry mouth; skin reaction

biomarker_match.rationale: t(11;18)+ MALT lymphomas are radiation-sensitive despite being antibiotic-resistant; complete remission achieved in all t(11;18)+ cases after RT in published series; ideal for this molecular profile

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Low-dose involved-field radiation therapy (24-30 Gy) targets the parotid gland containing MALT lymphoma. Radiation induces DNA damage in lymphoma cells, leading to apoptosis. MALT lymphoma is highly radiosensitive, with near-100% local control rates at modest doses. The entire parotid gland including the deep lobe should be included in the CTV.

key_evidence.study_name: Long-term outcome in localized EMZL treated with radiotherapy

notable_side_effects: Potential contralateral parotid sparing with IMRT

treatments.name: Rituximab Monotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple retrospective series and prospective studies in Sjogren-associated MALT lymphoma; 88% complete clinical response; well-established in practice though no randomized comparison with RT

survival_benefit.rationale: Complete clinical response 88%, histological CR 71%; dual benefit of treating both lymphoma and Sjogren's autoimmune disease activity; some patients require retreatment

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved anti-CD20 antibody widely available; IV infusion every week x4 or q3mo maintenance; well-established dosing protocols

safety_profile.rationale: Generally well-tolerated; infusion reactions manageable with premedication; low infection risk; avoids radiation-induced xerostomia (important in Sjogren's patient)

biomarker_match.rationale: CD20+ is required and confirmed in this patient; rituximab targets CD20 on MALT lymphoma B-cells; also treats underlying Sjogren's B-cell hyperactivity; dual therapeutic benefit

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Rituximab is a chimeric anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody that depletes CD20-positive B-cells through complement-dependent cytotoxicity (CDC), antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC), and direct apoptosis induction. In Sjogren-associated MALT lymphoma, it provides dual benefit by eliminating lymphoma B-cells and reducing autoimmune B-cell activity.

key_evidence.study_name: Rituximab therapy for MALT lymphomas of the parotid gland in Sjogren's disease
key_evidence.journal: Revue de Medecine Interne / Journal of Rheumatology

treatments.name: Rituximab + Cyclophosphamide Combination

evidence_level.rationale: Retrospective and small prospective series in Sjogren-associated parotid MALT lymphoma; higher CR rate than rituximab alone (66% histological CR vs 50%); no large RCT

survival_benefit.rationale: 72% complete clinical remission at 24 months; superior histological CR rate over rituximab monotherapy; also improves Sjogren's disease activity markers including cryoglobulinemia

accessibility.rationale: Both agents widely available; cyclophosphamide is generic and inexpensive; standard dosing protocols established

safety_profile.rationale: Cyclophosphamide adds myelosuppression, infection risk, hemorrhagic cystitis risk, nausea; more toxic than rituximab alone; gonadotoxicity concern in younger patients

biomarker_match.rationale: CD20+ confirmed; combination may overcome BAFF-mediated rituximab resistance reported in Sjogren-MALT lymphoma; addresses both lymphoma and autoimmune B-cell dysregulation

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Rituximab depletes CD20+ B-cells while cyclophosphamide provides alkylating agent cytotoxicity, targeting rapidly dividing lymphoma cells and immunosuppressing the autoimmune B-cell population driving Sjogren's disease. The combination achieves deeper responses than rituximab monotherapy.

key_evidence.study_name: Efficacy of Rituximab and Cyclophosphamide in Primary Sjogren's Syndrome with Parotid MALT Lymphoma

treatments.name: Bendamustine + Rituximab (BR)

evidence_level.rationale: Large international consortium analysis (237 patients); ORR 93.2%, CR 81%; NCCN-recommended first-line immunochemotherapy for MZL requiring systemic treatment

survival_benefit.rationale: 7-year EFS 88%; 5-year PFS 94.4% with rituximab maintenance; overcomes known adverse prognostic factors; highly effective durable remissions

accessibility.rationale: Both agents widely available; bendamustine IV infusion; standard outpatient chemotherapy regimen; 6 cycles typical

safety_profile.rationale: Myelosuppression, herpes zoster reactivation (4%), infection risk (13%); immunosuppression may worsen Sjogren's susceptibility to infections; prophylactic antivirals recommended

biomarker_match.rationale: Effective across all MZL subtypes including EMZL; CD20+ confirmed; may be more appropriate for disseminated or symptomatic disease than for Stage IE

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Bendamustine is a bifunctional alkylating agent with purine analog properties that causes DNA cross-linking and strand breaks. Combined with rituximab (anti-CD20), the BR regimen provides synergistic anti-lymphoma activity with high complete response rates in extranodal marginal zone lymphoma.

key_evidence.study_name: International analysis of frontline BR in EMZL / MALT2008-01 trial

treatments.name: Watch and Wait (Active Surveillance)

evidence_level.rationale: Established approach for asymptomatic indolent lymphomas; NCCN recommends observation for select asymptomatic low-burden MZL; long natural history of MALT lymphoma supports watchful waiting

survival_benefit.rationale: No survival difference between immediate treatment and deferred treatment in asymptomatic indolent lymphomas; avoids treatment toxicity; MALT lymphoma has excellent prognosis regardless of timing

accessibility.rationale: No treatment required; regular clinical and imaging follow-up; blood work monitoring; available universally

safety_profile.rationale: No treatment toxicity; avoids radiation-induced xerostomia in already dry mouth from Sjogren's; no immunosuppression risk

biomarker_match.rationale: t(11;18)+ tumors may have a tendency to disseminate and should be monitored closely; Stage IE is favorable but t(11;18) status warrants consideration of early treatment

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Active surveillance with regular clinical examinations, laboratory monitoring (CBC, LDH, serum protein electrophoresis), and periodic imaging. MALT lymphoma is an indolent disease with excellent long-term survival, and treatment can be deferred until symptoms or progression occur.

key_evidence.study_name: Various observational studies of indolent lymphoma watch and wait

notable_side_effects: Risk of disease progression if not monitored closely

treatments.name: Zanubrutinib (BTK Inhibitor)

evidence_level.rationale: MAGNOLIA Phase 2 trial: ORR 68.2%, CR 25.8% in relapsed/refractory MZL; FDA accelerated approval for R/R MZL after prior anti-CD20 therapy; growing evidence for frontline use

survival_benefit.rationale: Durable responses in R/R MZL; ORR 68.2% with 25.8% CR; responses consistent across MZL subtypes; oral targeted therapy with sustained disease control

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for R/R MZL after prior anti-CD20 therapy; oral medication; easier administration than IV regimens; approved in second-line setting

safety_profile.rationale: More selective BTK inhibitor with fewer off-target effects than ibrutinib; lower rates of atrial fibrillation and bleeding; bruising, diarrhea, upper respiratory infections common

biomarker_match.rationale: Effective across MZL subtypes; BTK pathway relevant to B-cell survival in MALT lymphoma; less data on t(11;18)+ specifically; generally applicable to B-cell lymphomas

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Zanubrutinib is a next-generation Bruton tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitor with high selectivity for BTK. It blocks B-cell receptor signaling, NF-kB activation, and B-cell survival pathways critical for marginal zone lymphoma proliferation, with improved selectivity reducing off-target toxicities compared to first-generation BTK inhibitors.
biomarker_requirements: Prior anti-CD20 therapy for approved indication

treatments.name: Ibrutinib (First-Generation BTK Inhibitor)

evidence_level.rationale: FDA-approved for R/R MZL in 2017; Phase 2 data showing ORR 48% with 3% CR; established in clinical practice for second-line MZL

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 48%, CR 3% in R/R MZL; median PFS 14.2 months; less impressive than zanubrutinib; approved but increasingly superseded by next-generation BTK inhibitors

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for R/R MZL; oral daily medication; widely available; prior anti-CD20 therapy required for approved indication

safety_profile.rationale: Higher rates of atrial fibrillation (6-16%), bleeding, hypertension compared to zanubrutinib; more off-target kinase inhibition; autoimmune complications possible in Sjogren's patients

biomarker_match.rationale: BTK pathway blockade relevant to MZL biology; less selective than zanubrutinib; off-target effects may be more problematic in Sjogren's autoimmune background

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Ibrutinib irreversibly inhibits Bruton tyrosine kinase (BTK), blocking B-cell receptor signaling and NF-kB pathway activation essential for marginal zone lymphoma survival. Less selective than zanubrutinib, with additional off-target inhibition of ITK, EGFR, and TEC family kinases.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2 Study of Ibrutinib in R/R MZL

biomarker_requirements: Prior anti-CD20 therapy for approved indication

treatments.name: Chlorambucil + Rituximab

evidence_level.rationale: IELSG-19 Phase 3 trial in EMZL; chlorambucil-rituximab superior to chlorambucil alone; NCCN-listed immunochemotherapy option for MZL

survival_benefit.rationale: Superior EFS vs chlorambucil alone; well-tolerated oral alkylator combined with anti-CD20; adequate for Stage IE parotid MALT lymphoma

accessibility.rationale: Chlorambucil is oral, generic, inexpensive; rituximab widely available; easy outpatient administration; minimal infrastructure requirements

safety_profile.rationale: Mild myelosuppression; generally well-tolerated; oral administration convenient; less toxic than bendamustine-based regimens; suitable for older or comorbid patients

biomarker_match.rationale: CD20+ confirmed; effective in EMZL; oral alkylator provides convenient long-term immunosuppression; may help control Sjogren's B-cell activity

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Chlorambucil is an oral alkylating agent that cross-links DNA, inhibiting cell division in lymphoma cells. Combined with rituximab (anti-CD20 antibody), it provides dual anti-lymphoma activity with convenient oral administration and well-established tolerability.

treatments.name: Mosunetuzumab (CD20xCD3 Bispecific Antibody)

evidence_level.rationale: FDA-approved for R/R follicular lymphoma; Phase 2/3 trials ongoing in MZL (NCT06006117); no specific MZL approval yet but promising class

survival_benefit.rationale: High response rates in R/R indolent lymphomas; potential for deep responses in MZL; time-limited treatment (fixed-duration dosing); results in MZL pending

accessibility.rationale: Approved for FL but not yet for MZL; clinical trial access for MZL; subcutaneous formulation improving convenience

safety_profile.rationale: Cytokine release syndrome (CRS) risk with first doses; neurological toxicity possible; step-up dosing mitigates CRS; better long-term profile than continuous BTK inhibition

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets CD20 on lymphoma cells and CD3 on T-cells; bridges immune effectors directly to tumor cells; CD20+ confirmed in this patient

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Mosunetuzumab is a CD20xCD3 bispecific antibody that simultaneously binds CD20 on lymphoma B-cells and CD3 on T-cells, redirecting T-cell cytotoxicity against lymphoma cells. This bispecific approach generates potent anti-lymphoma immune responses independent of natural T-cell receptor specificity.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 3 Mosunetuzumab-Lenalidomide vs Investigator's Choice in R/R MZL (NCT06006117)

treatments.name: Pirtobrutinib + Rituximab (Non-Covalent BTK Inhibitor)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 trial ongoing for untreated MZL; pirtobrutinib is non-covalent BTK inhibitor with activity in covalent BTK inhibitor-resistant disease; novel mechanism

survival_benefit.rationale: Pirtobrutinib showed activity in BTK inhibitor-resistant lymphomas; combination with rituximab in treatment-naive MZL may provide high response rates; data pending

accessibility.rationale: Clinical trial access only for MZL; pirtobrutinib FDA-approved for MCL; oral medication

safety_profile.rationale: Non-covalent BTK inhibitor with potentially lower off-target toxicity; lower rates of atrial fibrillation and bleeding compared to covalent BTK inhibitors

biomarker_match.rationale: BTK pathway relevant to MZL biology; novel mechanism may overcome resistance to covalent BTK inhibitors; applicability to t(11;18)+ MALT under investigation

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pirtobrutinib is a highly selective, non-covalent (reversible) BTK inhibitor that retains activity against BTK C481S mutations conferring resistance to covalent BTK inhibitors. Combined with rituximab, it provides dual-mechanism anti-lymphoma therapy with potentially improved safety over first-generation BTK inhibitors.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2 Pirtobrutinib + Rituximab in Untreated MZL (University of Utah)

treatments.name: Rituximab + Ibrutinib Combination

evidence_level.rationale: ICML 2025 poster presentation showing promise in MZL; combining anti-CD20 with BTK inhibition; early data encouraging but not yet practice-changing

survival_benefit.rationale: Combination expected to improve response rates and durability over either agent alone; synergistic B-cell targeting through different mechanisms

accessibility.rationale: Both agents FDA-approved individually; combination use off-label for MZL; oral + IV regimen

safety_profile.rationale: Combined toxicities of both agents; ibrutinib cardiac and bleeding risks; rituximab infusion reactions; monitoring for atrial fibrillation essential

biomarker_match.rationale: CD20+ confirmed for rituximab; BTK pathway relevant; combination addresses both direct cytotoxicity and signaling pathway blockade

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Combining rituximab (anti-CD20 direct cell killing) with ibrutinib (BTK pathway inhibition) provides dual-mechanism anti-lymphoma therapy. Rituximab-mediated ADCC and CDC complement ibrutinib's blockade of B-cell receptor survival signaling.

treatments.name: Ultra-Low-Dose Radiation Therapy (4 Gy in 2 fractions)

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple studies demonstrating high response rates with ultra-low-dose RT (4 Gy/2 fractions) in indolent lymphomas; NCCN-recommended option for palliative or localized disease

survival_benefit.rationale: High initial response rate but lower durability than standard-dose RT; ORR 80-90% but local recurrence higher; may need retreatment; best as palliative option

accessibility.rationale: Simplest radiation regimen possible; 2 fractions only; completed in 2 days; minimal patient burden; available everywhere

safety_profile.rationale: Minimal toxicity with only 4 Gy total dose; negligible xerostomia risk; essentially no acute or late side effects; ideal for preserving residual parotid function in Sjogren's

biomarker_match.rationale: MALT lymphoma is radiosensitive; however, t(11;18)+ may need standard-dose RT for durable control; ultra-low dose is less proven in this molecular subset

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Ultra-low-dose radiation therapy (2 x 2 Gy) induces apoptosis in highly radiosensitive lymphoma cells with minimal damage to surrounding tissue. This approach capitalizes on the extreme radiosensitivity of indolent B-cell lymphomas while virtually eliminating radiation toxicity.

key_evidence.study_name: Ultra-Low-Dose RT for Indolent Lymphomas (Multiple series)

notable_side_effects: Minimal — virtually none at 4 Gy total dose

treatments.name: Lenalidomide + Rituximab (R2)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2/3 data in MZL; AUGMENT trial included MZL patients; FDA-approved for R/R MZL in combination; oral immunomodulatory agent

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR approximately 56% in MZL subset of AUGMENT trial; PFS improvement over rituximab alone; oral combination convenient

accessibility.rationale: Lenalidomide FDA-approved for R/R MZL with rituximab; REMS program for teratogenicity risk; oral + IV combination

safety_profile.rationale: Myelosuppression, infection risk, DVT/PE risk, tumor flare; teratogenicity requires REMS; autoimmune flare concern in Sjogren's patient

biomarker_match.rationale: CD20+ confirmed; immunomodulatory mechanism may help control B-cell dysregulation; concern about autoimmune flare in Sjogren's background

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Lenalidomide is an immunomodulatory drug (IMiD) that enhances T-cell and NK-cell function, promotes antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity, and inhibits tumor cell proliferation through cereblon-mediated degradation of IKZF1/3. Combined with rituximab, it provides immune-mediated anti-lymphoma activity.

clinical_trials.title: Phase 3 Mosunetuzumab-Lenalidomide vs Investigator's Choice in R/R MZL
clinical_trials.early_results: Bispecific antibody + IMiD combination; results pending; may establish new standard for R/R MZL

clinical_trials.title: Obinutuzumab + Glofitamab in MZL
clinical_trials.early_results: Bispecific antibody combination; leveraging glofitamab's proven activity in aggressive B-cell lymphomas for indolent histologies

clinical_trials.title: Pirtobrutinib + Rituximab in Untreated MZL
clinical_trials.early_results: Non-covalent BTK inhibitor combination; first-line approach; results pending

clinical_trials.title: Radiation Therapy Alone for Stage 1 and 2 MALT Lymphoma
clinical_trials.early_results: Establishing optimal radiation dose and fractionation for early-stage MALT lymphoma

clinical_trials.title: Odronextamab Monotherapy in R/R B-cell NHL including MZL
clinical_trials.early_results: CD20xCD3 bispecific antibody; MZL cohort included; results pending

clinical_trials.title: Rituximab + Ibrutinib Combination in MZL
clinical_trials.early_results: Promising preliminary data presented at ICML 2025; combination of anti-CD20 + BTK inhibition

combination_strategies.rationale: For Stage IE parotid MALT lymphoma with t(11;18)+, rituximab addresses systemic CD20+ B-cell disease and Sjogren's autoimmune activity while RT provides definitive local control; combination offers both local cure and systemic B-cell modulation

R-cyclophosphamide achieves higher histological CR rate (66%) vs rituximab alone (50%) in Sjogren-associated parotid MALT; dual benefit for lymphoma and autoimmune disease; addresses BAFF-mediated rituximab resistance
BR achieves 93% ORR with 81% CR; rituximab maintenance improves 5-year PFS from 81% to 94%; most effective immunochemotherapy for EMZL; reserved for symptomatic or disseminated disease
Dual-pathway anti-lymphoma therapy combining direct CD20 targeting with BTK pathway inhibition; may achieve deeper responses than either agent alone; being evaluated in ongoing trials

Coordinated management of underlying autoimmune disease reduces ongoing B-cell stimulation that drives lymphomagenesis; hydroxychloroquine may reduce lymphoma risk; rituximab treats both conditions simultaneously

supportive_care.evidence: ACR/EULAR Sjogren's management guidelines; coordinated rheumatology-hematology care essential for Sjogren-associated MALT lymphoma
supportive_care.benefit: Controlling autoimmune B-cell stimulation reduces ongoing lymphomagenesis risk; managing dry eyes/mouth improves quality of life; monitoring for disease activity that may predict lymphoma recurrence

supportive_care.evidence: RCTs showing pilocarpine and cevimeline improve salivary flow in Sjogren's; particularly important if radiation therapy further damages parotid function
supportive_care.benefit: Improves oral comfort, reduces dental caries, enhances quality of life; especially important if involved-field RT to parotid gland worsens existing xerostomia from Sjogren's

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN/ASCO guidelines mandate HBV screening before anti-CD20 therapy; reactivation risk with rituximab-based treatment; entecavir prophylaxis if HBsAg or anti-HBc positive
supportive_care.benefit: Prevents potentially fatal hepatitis B reactivation during immunosuppressive anti-CD20 therapy; prophylactic antivirals significantly reduce reactivation risk

supportive_care.evidence: Cryoglobulinemia is a high-risk biomarker for lymphoma development and recurrence in Sjogren's; monitoring cryoglobulins, complement levels, and rheumatoid factor
supportive_care.benefit: Early detection of biomarker changes that predict lymphoma recurrence or transformation; cryoglobulin clearance may indicate treatment response

supportive_care.approach: Dental Care and Oral Hygiene Intensification
supportive_care.evidence: Standard supportive care for Sjogren's patients with xerostomia; fluoride rinses, regular dental examinations, sugar-free gum stimulation
supportive_care.benefit: Prevents dental caries and oral infections worsened by xerostomia; especially critical if radiation therapy to parotid region further reduces saliva production

supportive_care.evidence: Herpes zoster reactivation reported in 4% of patients on bendamustine-rituximab; PJP prophylaxis recommended with certain immunochemotherapy regimens
supportive_care.benefit: Prevents opportunistic infections during immunosuppressive lymphoma treatment; acyclovir/valacyclovir prophylaxis reduces herpes zoster risk
